sanskrit: व्यासप्रसादाच्छ्रुतवानेतद्गुह्यं अहं परम्। योगं योगेश्वरात्कृष्णात्साक्षात्कथयतः स्वयम्॥
transliteration: vyāsa-prasādāc chrutavān etad guhyam ahaṁ param yogaṁ yogeśvarāt kṛṣṇāt sākṣāt kathayataḥ svayam
literal_meaning: By Vyāsa’s grace I heard this supreme secret, the yoga from the Lord of Yoga Kṛṣṇa Himself speaking directly.
contextual_meaning: Sañjaya expresses gratitude for receiving the highest psychological insight—direct perception of integrated Self—transmitted through an unbroken chain of mentors, validating that authentic wisdom is relationally mediated yet experientially immediate.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to a mentoring presence that allows full internalization of self-transcendent knowledge.
trigger_scenario: A supervisee finishing therapy training realizes they have ‘heard’ the healing presence of their supervisor and can now trust their own clinical intuition.
anchor_text: True wisdom is heard from the teacher and realized in the heart.
